name: hashline-edit
description: Hash-anchored line editing tools for GitHub Copilot — read and edit files using line numbers with content hashes for reliable, token-efficient edits.

languageModelTools.name: hashline_read
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read file lines with content hashes for verified editing
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read lines from a file with line numbers and content hashes. Each line is formatted as `{lineNumber}:{twoLetterHash}|{content}`. Use these hashes with `hashline_edit` to make verified edits. The hash is a 2-letter content fingerprint — if the file changes, the hashes change, and edits will be safely rejected.

filePath.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to the file to read.

startLine.description: First line to read (1-based). Defaults to 1.

endLine.description: Last line to read (1-based, inclusive). Defaults to last line of file.

languageModelTools.name: hashline_edit
languageModelTools.userDescription: Edit files using hash-verified line references
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Edit file(s) by specifying line ranges with content hashes from `hashline_read`. Supports replace, insert-after, and delete. Each edit specifies `lineHashes` (comma-separated `{line}:{hash}` pairs) and `content` (new text). Set `content` to empty string to delete lines. Set `insertAfter: true` to insert after the anchor line. Multiple edits can be batched; successful edits are kept even if others fail.

edits.description: Array of edit operations to apply.

filePath.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to the file.

lineHashes.description: Comma-separated line:hash pairs copied from hashline_read output, e.g. '10:qk,11:ab,12:fn'. For insert at file start, use '0:'.

content.description: New content to replace the identified lines. Use empty string to delete. May contain newlines for multi-line content.

insertAfter.description: If true, insert content after the anchor line instead of replacing it. lineHashes should identify the anchor line.
